# 016 — Terraform AWS EC2 with SSH Key Generated by Terraform

**By:** Saravanan Sundaramoorthy
**Environment:** Ubuntu Linux (GCE VM)
**Time:** ~25 minutes

## Topic

This demo builds on **014.1** (EC2 + manual SSH key). The key difference:

**014.1** — you run `ssh-keygen` yourself, then Terraform uploads your key.
**016** — Terraform generates the key pair using the `hashicorp/tls` provider, saves the private key to disk, and uploads the public key to AWS. No manual `ssh-keygen` needed.

This shows how to use:

- `tls_private_key` — generates an ED25519 key pair inside Terraform
- `local_sensitive_file` — writes the private key to disk with `0600` permissions
- `aws_key_pair` — registers the generated public key with AWS
- `data "aws_ami"` — auto-selects the latest Ubuntu 22.04 AMI
- `aws_security_group` + `aws_instance` — same as before

**Why `local_sensitive_file` and not `local_file`?**
The private key is a secret. `local_sensitive_file` marks the content as sensitive so Terraform never prints it in plan or apply output.

---

## What Terraform Creates

```text
tls_private_key.demo          → generates ED25519 key pair in memory
local_sensitive_file.private_key → writes private key to ~/.ssh/terraform-016-demo
aws_key_pair.demo             → uploads public key to AWS
aws_security_group.ssh        → allows SSH on port 22
aws_instance.demo             → Ubuntu 22.04 EC2 instance
```

**Plan: 5 to add, 0 to change, 0 to destroy.**

---

## 1. Create Project Folder

```bash
mkdir -p ~/terraform-aws-ec2-016-demo
cd ~/terraform-aws-ec2-016-demo
```

---

## 2. Check Your AWS Region

```bash
aws configure get region
aws sts get-caller-identity
```

Update `terraform.tfvars` to match your configured region (e.g., `ap-south-1`).

---

## 3. Create Terraform Files

Create the following files:

```text
providers.tf
variables.tf
main.tf
outputs.tf
terraform.tfvars
```

---

## 4. providers.tf

Three providers are required:

```bash
cat > providers.tf <<'EOF_TF'
terraform {
  required_providers {
    aws = {
      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
      version = "~> 6.0"
    }
    tls = {
      source  = "hashicorp/tls"
      version = "~> 4.0"
    }
    local = {
      source  = "hashicorp/local"
      version = "~> 2.0"
    }
  }
}

provider "aws" {
  region = var.aws_region
}
EOF_TF
```

| Provider | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `hashicorp/aws` | Creates AWS resources |
| `hashicorp/tls` | Generates the SSH key pair |
| `hashicorp/local` | Saves the private key file to disk |

---

## 5. variables.tf

```bash
cat > variables.tf <<'EOF_TF'
variable "aws_region" {
  description = "AWS region"
  type        = string
  default     = "us-east-1"
}

variable "instance_type" {
  description = "EC2 instance type"
  type        = string
  default     = "t3.micro"
}

variable "key_name" {
  description = "AWS EC2 key pair name"
  type        = string
  default     = "terraform-016-demo-key"
}

variable "private_key_path" {
  description = "Local path where Terraform saves the generated private key"
  type        = string
  default     = "~/.ssh/terraform-016-demo"
}

variable "allowed_ssh_cidr" {
  description = "CIDR block allowed to SSH into EC2"
  type        = string
  default     = "0.0.0.0/0"
}

variable "instance_name" {
  description = "Name tag for the EC2 instance"
  type        = string
  default     = "terraform-016-demo-ec2"
}
EOF_TF
```

Notice: there is **no `public_key_path` variable** here. Unlike 014.1, you do not point Terraform at a pre-existing key file. Terraform creates the key itself.

---

## 6. main.tf

```bash
cat > main.tf <<'EOF_TF'
# Generate SSH key pair using Terraform TLS provider
resource "tls_private_key" "demo" {
  algorithm = "ED25519"
}

# Save private key to local disk
resource "local_sensitive_file" "private_key" {
  content         = tls_private_key.demo.private_key_openssh
  filename        = pathexpand(var.private_key_path)
  file_permission = "0600"
}

# Fetch latest Ubuntu 22.04 LTS AMI from Canonical
data "aws_ami" "ubuntu" {
  most_recent = true
  owners      = ["099720109477"]

  filter {
    name   = "name"
    values = ["ubuntu/images/hvm-ssd/ubuntu-jammy-22.04-amd64-server-*"]
  }

  filter {
    name   = "virtualization-type"
    values = ["hvm"]
  }

  filter {
    name   = "state"
    values = ["available"]
  }
}

# Register Terraform-generated public key as AWS EC2 Key Pair
resource "aws_key_pair" "demo" {
  key_name   = var.key_name
  public_key = tls_private_key.demo.public_key_openssh
}

# Security group to allow SSH access
resource "aws_security_group" "ssh" {
  name        = "terraform-016-ssh-sg"
  description = "Allow SSH access to EC2 instance"

  ingress {
    description = "SSH from allowed CIDR"
    from_port   = 22
    to_port     = 22
    protocol    = "tcp"
    cidr_blocks = [var.allowed_ssh_cidr]
  }

  egress {
    description = "Allow all outbound traffic"
    from_port   = 0
    to_port     = 0
    protocol    = "-1"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "terraform-016-ssh-sg"
  }
}

# Create EC2 instance
resource "aws_instance" "demo" {
  ami                         = data.aws_ami.ubuntu.id
  instance_type               = var.instance_type
  key_name                    = aws_key_pair.demo.key_name
  vpc_security_group_ids      = [aws_security_group.ssh.id]
  associate_public_ip_address = true

  tags = {
    Name = var.instance_name
  }
}
EOF_TF
```

**Key connections in main.tf:**

```text
tls_private_key.demo.private_key_openssh  → local_sensitive_file (saved to disk)
tls_private_key.demo.public_key_openssh   → aws_key_pair.demo.public_key (uploaded to AWS)
aws_key_pair.demo.key_name                → aws_instance.demo.key_name
aws_security_group.ssh.id                 → aws_instance.demo.vpc_security_group_ids
data.aws_ami.ubuntu.id                    → aws_instance.demo.ami
```

---

## 7. outputs.tf

```bash
cat > outputs.tf <<'EOF_TF'
output "ubuntu_ami_id" {
  description = "Ubuntu AMI selected by Terraform"
  value       = data.aws_ami.ubuntu.id
}

output "instance_id" {
  description = "EC2 instance ID"
  value       = aws_instance.demo.id
}

output "instance_public_ip" {
  description = "Public IP address of EC2 instance"
  value       = aws_instance.demo.public_ip
}

output "private_key_path" {
  description = "Path to the Terraform-generated private key on disk"
  value       = local_sensitive_file.private_key.filename
}

output "ssh_command" {
  description = "SSH command to connect to EC2"
  value       = "ssh -i ${pathexpand(var.private_key_path)} ubuntu@${aws_instance.demo.public_ip}"
}
EOF_TF
```

---

## 8. terraform.tfvars

```bash
cat > terraform.tfvars <<'EOF_TF'
aws_region       = "ap-south-1"
instance_type    = "t3.micro"
key_name         = "terraform-016-demo-key"
allowed_ssh_cidr = "0.0.0.0/0"
instance_name    = "terraform-016-demo-ec2"
private_key_path = "~/.ssh/terraform-016-demo"
EOF_TF
```

Update `aws_region` to match your configured region (`aws configure get region`).

---

## 9. Safer SSH CIDR (Optional)

Instead of allowing SSH from anywhere:

```bash
MY_IP=$(curl -s ifconfig.me)
echo "My IP: $MY_IP"
```

Update `terraform.tfvars`:

```hcl
allowed_ssh_cidr = "YOUR_IP/32"
```

---

## 10. Initialize Terraform

```bash
terraform init
```

Expected output:

```text
- Installing hashicorp/aws v6.x.x...
- Installing hashicorp/tls v4.x.x...
- Installing hashicorp/local v2.x.x...

Terraform has been successfully initialized!
```

All three providers are downloaded and installed.

---

## 11. Format and Validate

```bash
terraform fmt
terraform validate
```

Expected:

```text
Success! The configuration is valid.
```

---

## 12. Plan

```bash
terraform plan
```

Expected plan output:

```text
# tls_private_key.demo will be created
  + resource "tls_private_key" "demo" {
      + algorithm           = "ED25519"
      + private_key_openssh = (sensitive value)
      + private_key_pem     = (sensitive value)
      + public_key_openssh  = (known after apply)
    }

# local_sensitive_file.private_key will be created
  + resource "local_sensitive_file" "private_key" {
      + content         = (sensitive value)
      + file_permission = "0600"
      + filename        = "/home/USER/.ssh/terraform-016-demo"
    }

# aws_key_pair.demo will be created
# aws_security_group.ssh will be created
# aws_instance.demo will be created

Plan: 5 to add, 0 to change, 0 to destroy.
```

Note that `private_key_openssh` is always shown as `(sensitive value)` — Terraform never prints the private key in any output.

---

## 13. Apply

```bash
terraform apply
```

Type `yes` when prompted.

Expected output after apply:

```text
tls_private_key.demo: Creating...
tls_private_key.demo: Creation complete after 0s
local_sensitive_file.private_key: Creating...
local_sensitive_file.private_key: Creation complete after 0s
aws_key_pair.demo: Creating...
aws_security_group.ssh: Creating...
aws_key_pair.demo: Creation complete after 0s
aws_security_group.ssh: Creation complete after 2s
aws_instance.demo: Creating...
aws_instance.demo: Creation complete after 12s

Apply complete! Resources: 5 added, 0 changed, 0 destroyed.

Outputs:

instance_id        = "i-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
instance_public_ip = "xx.xx.xx.xx"
private_key_path   = "/home/USER/.ssh/terraform-016-demo"
ssh_command        = "ssh -i /home/USER/.ssh/terraform-016-demo ubuntu@xx.xx.xx.xx"
ubuntu_ami_id      = "ami-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
```

**Creation order matters:**
1. `tls_private_key` is created first (no dependencies)
2. `local_sensitive_file` waits for the private key
3. `aws_key_pair` waits for the public key
4. `aws_security_group` is independent, runs in parallel with `aws_key_pair`
5. `aws_instance` waits for both key pair and security group

---

## 14. Verify Private Key on Disk

After apply, confirm the private key was saved:

```bash
ls -la ~/.ssh/terraform-016-demo
```

Expected:

```text
-rw------- 1 USER USER 387 DATE /home/USER/.ssh/terraform-016-demo
```

Check it is a valid OpenSSH key:

```bash
file ~/.ssh/terraform-016-demo
```

Expected:

```text
/home/USER/.ssh/terraform-016-demo: OpenSSH private key
```

The `0600` permission is set automatically by `local_sensitive_file`. No manual `chmod` needed.

---

## 15. SSH into EC2

Get the SSH command from outputs:

```bash
terraform output -raw ssh_command
```

Run it directly:

```bash
$(terraform output -raw ssh_command)
```

Or use the key path and IP manually:

```bash
ssh -i ~/.ssh/terraform-016-demo ubuntu@$(terraform output -raw instance_public_ip)
```

When prompted about host fingerprint, type `yes`.

Check system info inside EC2:

```bash
whoami
hostname
lsb_release -a
```

Expected:

```text
ubuntu
ip-172-xx-xx-xx
Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS
```

---

## 16. Get Outputs Again Later

```bash
terraform output
terraform output -raw ssh_command
terraform output private_key_path
```

---

## 17. Destroy Resources

After the demo, remove all AWS resources and the local private key:

```bash
terraform destroy
```

Type `yes`.

After destroy, Terraform also deletes the local private key file (`local_sensitive_file` is managed, so destroy removes it too):

```bash
ls ~/.ssh/terraform-016-demo
# ls: cannot access '...': No such file or directory
```

---

## 18. Full Copy-Paste Setup Script

```bash
mkdir -p ~/terraform-aws-ec2-016-demo
cd ~/terraform-aws-ec2-016-demo

cat > providers.tf <<'EOF_TF'
terraform {
  required_providers {
    aws = {
      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
      version = "~> 6.0"
    }
    tls = {
      source  = "hashicorp/tls"
      version = "~> 4.0"
    }
    local = {
      source  = "hashicorp/local"
      version = "~> 2.0"
    }
  }
}

provider "aws" {
  region = var.aws_region
}
EOF_TF

cat > variables.tf <<'EOF_TF'
variable "aws_region" {
  description = "AWS region"
  type        = string
  default     = "us-east-1"
}

variable "instance_type" {
  description = "EC2 instance type"
  type        = string
  default     = "t3.micro"
}

variable "key_name" {
  description = "AWS EC2 key pair name"
  type        = string
  default     = "terraform-016-demo-key"
}

variable "private_key_path" {
  description = "Local path where Terraform saves the generated private key"
  type        = string
  default     = "~/.ssh/terraform-016-demo"
}

variable "allowed_ssh_cidr" {
  description = "CIDR block allowed to SSH into EC2"
  type        = string
  default     = "0.0.0.0/0"
}

variable "instance_name" {
  description = "Name tag for the EC2 instance"
  type        = string
  default     = "terraform-016-demo-ec2"
}
EOF_TF

cat > main.tf <<'EOF_TF'
resource "tls_private_key" "demo" {
  algorithm = "ED25519"
}

resource "local_sensitive_file" "private_key" {
  content         = tls_private_key.demo.private_key_openssh
  filename        = pathexpand(var.private_key_path)
  file_permission = "0600"
}

data "aws_ami" "ubuntu" {
  most_recent = true
  owners      = ["099720109477"]

  filter {
    name   = "name"
    values = ["ubuntu/images/hvm-ssd/ubuntu-jammy-22.04-amd64-server-*"]
  }

  filter {
    name   = "virtualization-type"
    values = ["hvm"]
  }

  filter {
    name   = "state"
    values = ["available"]
  }
}

resource "aws_key_pair" "demo" {
  key_name   = var.key_name
  public_key = tls_private_key.demo.public_key_openssh
}

resource "aws_security_group" "ssh" {
  name        = "terraform-016-ssh-sg"
  description = "Allow SSH access to EC2 instance"

  ingress {
    description = "SSH from allowed CIDR"
    from_port   = 22
    to_port     = 22
    protocol    = "tcp"
    cidr_blocks = [var.allowed_ssh_cidr]
  }

  egress {
    description = "Allow all outbound traffic"
    from_port   = 0
    to_port     = 0
    protocol    = "-1"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
  }

  tags = {
    Name = "terraform-016-ssh-sg"
  }
}

resource "aws_instance" "demo" {
  ami                         = data.aws_ami.ubuntu.id
  instance_type               = var.instance_type
  key_name                    = aws_key_pair.demo.key_name
  vpc_security_group_ids      = [aws_security_group.ssh.id]
  associate_public_ip_address = true

  tags = {
    Name = var.instance_name
  }
}
EOF_TF

cat > outputs.tf <<'EOF_TF'
output "ubuntu_ami_id" {
  description = "Ubuntu AMI selected by Terraform"
  value       = data.aws_ami.ubuntu.id
}

output "instance_id" {
  description = "EC2 instance ID"
  value       = aws_instance.demo.id
}

output "instance_public_ip" {
  description = "Public IP address of EC2 instance"
  value       = aws_instance.demo.public_ip
}

output "private_key_path" {
  description = "Path to the Terraform-generated private key on disk"
  value       = local_sensitive_file.private_key.filename
}

output "ssh_command" {
  description = "SSH command to connect to EC2"
  value       = "ssh -i ${pathexpand(var.private_key_path)} ubuntu@${aws_instance.demo.public_ip}"
}
EOF_TF

MY_REGION=$(aws configure get region 2>/dev/null || echo "us-east-1")

cat > terraform.tfvars <<EOF_TF
aws_region       = "${MY_REGION}"
instance_type    = "t3.micro"
key_name         = "terraform-016-demo-key"
allowed_ssh_cidr = "0.0.0.0/0"
instance_name    = "terraform-016-demo-ec2"
private_key_path = "~/.ssh/terraform-016-demo"
EOF_TF

terraform init
terraform fmt
terraform validate
terraform plan
```

Then apply:

```bash
terraform apply
```

---

## 19. 014.1 vs 016 Comparison

| Step | 014.1 | 016 |
|------|-------|-----|
| SSH key creation | `ssh-keygen` manually before Terraform | `tls_private_key` resource inside Terraform |
| Key saved to disk | You run `ssh-keygen`, key exists already | `local_sensitive_file` resource writes it |
| `public_key_path` variable | Required (points to `~/.ssh/xxx.pub`) | Not needed |
| `private_key_path` variable | Not needed | Required (tells Terraform where to write) |
| Number of resources | 3 (key_pair, sg, instance) | 5 (tls_key, local_file, key_pair, sg, instance) |
| Key in tfstate | Public key only | Private key stored in tfstate (sensitive) |
| Key deleted on destroy | Key file stays on disk | Key file deleted on destroy |

**Security note:** When Terraform generates the key via `tls_private_key`, the private key is stored in `terraform.tfstate`. Treat your state file as a secret — do not commit it to version control.

---

## 20. Common Errors

### Error: Key pair already exists

```text
InvalidKeyPair.Duplicate: The keypair 'terraform-016-demo-key' already exists.
```

Fix — delete it from AWS first:

```bash
aws ec2 delete-key-pair --key-name terraform-016-demo-key --region YOUR_REGION
```

Then apply again.

### Error: Private key file already exists

If `~/.ssh/terraform-016-demo` already exists on disk from a previous run, Terraform will overwrite it on next apply. The old key will no longer match the EC2 instance. Always destroy before re-applying when testing.

### Error: Permission denied (publickey)

```text
ubuntu@IP: Permission denied (publickey).
```

Possible causes:
1. You are using the wrong private key — check `terraform output -raw ssh_command`
2. The key file permissions are wrong — should be `0600`:

```bash
chmod 600 ~/.ssh/terraform-016-demo
```

3. You destroyed and re-applied — a new key was generated. SSH into the new IP with the current key.

### Error: SSH timeout

Instance not ready yet. Wait 20–30 seconds after apply and retry.

---

## 21. Final File Structure

```text
terraform-aws-ec2-016-demo/
├── main.tf
├── outputs.tf
├── providers.tf
├── terraform.tfvars
├── variables.tf
├── .terraform.lock.hcl
├── terraform.tfstate
└── terraform.tfstate.backup
```

Private key on disk (outside project folder):

```text
~/.ssh/terraform-016-demo       ← created by Terraform on apply, deleted on destroy
```

---

## 22. Concept Summary

| Resource / Concept | What It Does |
|---|---|
| `tls_private_key` | Generates an ED25519 key pair inside Terraform |
| `local_sensitive_file` | Writes private key to disk; marks content as sensitive |
| `file_permission = "0600"` | Correct SSH key permissions — set automatically |
| `pathexpand()` | Expands `~` to the full home directory path |
| `public_key_openssh` | The public key attribute used to register with AWS |
| `private_key_openssh` | The private key attribute saved to disk |
| `aws_key_pair` | Registers the public key with AWS EC2 |
| `(sensitive value)` | Terraform hides this from plan/apply output |
| `local_sensitive_file` on destroy | Removes the private key file from disk |
| tfstate security | Private key lives in tfstate — never commit it to git |
